T4:  b0(row0,col0), b1(row1,col0), b2(row2,col0), b3(row3,col0)  [second group]
T5:  (col1 implied)
T6:  (col2 implied)
T7:  b0(row0,col3), b1(row1,col3), b2(row2,col3), b3(row3,col3)
T8:  b0(row0,col0), b1(row1,col0), b2(row2,col0), b3(row3,col0)  [third group]
T9:  (col1 implied)
T10: (col2 implied)
T11: b0(row0,col3), b1(row1,col3), b2(row2,col3), b3(row3,col3)
T12: b0(row0,col0), b1(row1,col0), b2(row2,col0), b3(row3,col0)  [fourth group]
T13: (col1 implied)
T14: (col2 implied)
T15: b0(row0,col3), b1(row1,col3), b2(row2,col3), b3(row3,col3)
T16: b0(row0,col4), b1(row1,col4), b2(row2,col4), b3(row3,col4)
T17: (col5 implied)
T18: (col6 implied)
T19: b0(row0,col7), b1(row1,col7), b2(row2,col7), b3(row3,col7)
T20: b0(row0,col4), b1(row1,col4), b2(row2,col4), b3(row3,col4)
T21: (col5 implied)
T22: (col6 implied)
T23: b0(row0,col7), b1(row1,col7), b2(row2,col7), b3(row3,col7)
T24: b0(row0,col4), b1(row1,col4), b2(row2,col4), b3(row3,col4)
T25: (col5 implied)
T26: (col6 implied)
T27: b0(row0,col7), b1(row1,col7), b2(row2,col7), b3(row3,col7)
T28: b0(row0,col4), b1(row1,col4), b2(row2,col4), b3(row3,col4)
T29: (col5 implied)
T30: (col6 implied)
T31: b0(row0,col7), b1(row1,col7), b2(row2,col7), b3(row3,col7)

REGISTER PACKING DETAIL
Each thread holds 2 x 32-bit registers for matrix B:
Register 1 (low): b0 in bits [15:0], b1 in bits [31:16]  (two packed f16 values)
Register 2 (high): b2 in bits [15:0], b3 in bits [31:16]  (two packed f16 values)

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
Threads T0-T3 and T16-T19: MMA Computation 1 (columns 0-3 and 4-7 respectively)
Threads T4-T7 and T20-T23: MMA Computation 2 (columns 0-3 and 4-7 respectively)
Threads T8-T11 and T24-T27: MMA Computation 3 (columns 0-3 and 4-7 respectively)
Threads T12-T15 and T28-T31: MMA Computation 4 (columns 0-3 and 4-7 respectively)

Within each group of 4 threads (e.g., T0-T3), each thread covers one column of the B matrix, holding all K=4 row elements for that column. The arrows in the figure indicate that threads covering columns 0-3 (left half) and threads covering columns 4-7 (right half) participate in the same MMA computation together.
mma-884-C-f32-1.png:
MMA .m8n8k4 Matrix C/D Fragment Layout, .ctype = .f32

OVERVIEW:
- Matrix operation: MMA .m8n8k4
- Matrix: C / D (accumulator)
- Data type: .f32 (32-bit float)
- Each thread holds pairs of f32 elements (c0,c1), (c2,c3), (c4,c5), (c6,c7)
- The layout is split into two separate computations: MMA computation 1 and MMA computation 2
- Each computation table has rows R = 0..7 and columns C = 0..7 (column pairs: 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (R\C) represent matrix rows 0 through 7
- Columns represent matrix columns 0 through 7, grouped in pairs: cols 0-1, cols 2-3, cols 4-5, cols 6-7
- Each cell contains: Thread ID : {element_a, element_b}

---

MMA COMPUTATION 1 (covers threads T0, T1, T2, T3, T16, T17, T18, T19):

R\C | Cols 0-1          | Cols 2-3           | Cols 4-5           | Cols 6-7
----|-------------------|--------------------|--------------------|--------------------
0   | T0 : {c0, c1}     | T2 : {c0, c1}      | T0 : {c4, c5}      | T2 : {c4, c5}
1   | T1 : {c0, c1}     | T3 : {c0, c1}      | T1 : {c4, c5}      | T3 : {c4, c5}
2   | T0 : {c2, c3}     | T2 : {c2, c3}      | T0 : {c6, c7}      | T2 : {c6, c7}
3   | T1 : {c2, c3}     | T3 : {c2, c3}      | T1 : {c6, c7}      | T3 : {c6, c7}
4   | T16 : {c0, c1}    | T18 : {c0, c1}     | T16 : {c4, c5}     | T18 : {c4, c5}
5   | T17 : {c0, c1}    | T19 : {c0, c1}     | T17 : {c4, c5}     | T19 : {c4, c5}
6   | T16 : {c2, c3}    | T18 : {c2, c3}     | T16 : {c6, c7}     | T18 : {c6, c7}
7   | T17 : {c2, c3}    | T19 : {c2, c3}     | T17 : {c6, c7}     | T19 : {c6, c7}

---

MMA COMPUTATION 2 (covers threads T4, T5, T6, T7, T20, T21, T22, T23):

R\C | Cols 0-1          | Cols 2-3           | Cols 4-5           | Cols 6-7
----|-------------------|--------------------|--------------------|--------------------
0   | T4 : {c0, c1}     | T6 : {c0, c1}      | T4 : {c4, c5}      | T6 : {c4, c5}
1   | T5 : {c0, c1}     | T7 : {c0, c1}      | T5 : {c4, c5}      | T7 : {c4, c5}
2   | T4 : {c2, c3}     | T6 : {c2, c3}      | T4 : {c6, c7}      | T6 : {c6, c7}
3   | T5 : {c2, c3}     | T7 : {c2, c3}      | T5 : {c6, c7}      | T7 : {c6, c7}
4   | T20 : {c0, c1}    | T22 : {c0, c1}     | T20 : {c4, c5}     | T22 : {c4, c5}
5   | T21 : {c0, c1}    | T23 : {c0, c1}     | T21 : {c4, c5}     | T23 : {c4, c5}
6   | T20 : {c2, c3}    | T22 : {c2, c3}     | T20 : {c6, c7}     | T22 : {c6, c7}
7   | T21 : {c2, c3}    | T23 : {c2, c3}     | T21 : {c6, c7}     | T23 : {c6, c7}

---

REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING:
- Each thread holds 8 f32 registers for C/D: c0, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7
- Each f32 element occupies one full 32-bit register (no packing; one value per register)
- Elements are grouped in pairs within cells purely for display: {c0,c1}, {c2,c3}, {c4,c5}, {c6,c7}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERNS:

Computation 1:
- Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 handle matrix rows 0-3 (all 8 columns)
- Threads T16, T17, T18, T19 handle matrix rows 4-7 (all 8 columns)
- Within rows 0-3: T0 and T2 handle even rows (0, 2); T1 and T3 handle odd rows (1, 3)
- T0 handles cols 0-1 and 4-5; T2 handles cols 2-3 and 6-7 (for rows 0 and 2)
- T1 handles cols 0-1 and 4-5; T3 handles cols 2-3 and 6-7 (for rows 1 and 3)
- Same pattern repeated for T16/T17/T18/T19 in rows 4-7

Computation 2:
- Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 handle matrix rows 0-3 (all 8 columns)
- Threads T20, T21, T22, T23 handle matrix rows 4-7 (all 8 columns)
- Same structural pattern as Computation 1 but with thread IDs offset by 4 (T0->T4, T1->T5, T2->T6, T3->T7, T16->T20, T17->T21, T18->T22, T19->T23)

ELEMENT-TO-COLUMN MAPPING (per thread):
- c0, c1 -> matrix columns 0-1 (left half, upper row pair)
- c2, c3 -> matrix columns 0-1 (left half, lower row pair) OR columns 2-3
- c4, c5 -> matrix columns 4-5
- c6, c7 -> matrix columns 6-7

Specifically, within a thread's assigned rows:
- {c0, c1} corresponds to the first column group (cols 0-1 or 2-3 depending on thread) for the first two assigned rows
- {c2, c3} corresponds to the first column group for the next two assigned rows
- {c4, c5} corresponds to the second column group for the first two assigned rows
- {c6, c7} corresponds to the second column group for the next two assigned rows
mma-884-A-f64.png:
MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

MATRIX IDENTIFICATION:
- Instruction: MMA (Matrix Multiply-Accumulate)
- Shape: .m8n8k4 (M=8, N=8, K=4)
- Matrix: A
- Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 7)
- Columns represent column indices 0 through 3 (4 columns total, corresponding to K=4)
- Each cell contains one thread:register_element assignment
- Notation format: T{thread_id}:a{register_index}

FULL LAYOUT TABLE:
Row\Col | Col 0    | Col 1    | Col 2    | Col 3
--------|----------|----------|----------|----------
Row 0   | T0:a0    | T1:a0    | T2:a0    | T3:a0
Row 1   | T4:a0    | T5:a0    | T6:a0    | T7:a0
Row 2   | (arrow indicating continuation pattern from row 1 to row 7)
..      | (arrow indicating reverse/continuation)
Row 7   | T28:a0   | T29:a0   | T30:a0   | T31:a0

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (explicit entries):
- T0:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 0
- T1:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 1
- T2:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 2
- T3:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 3
- T4:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 0
- T5:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 1
- T6:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 2
- T7:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 3
- T28:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 0
- T29:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 1
- T30:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 2
- T31:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 3

INFERRED PATTERN (rows 2 through 6, implied by arrows):
- Row 2: T8:a0, T9:a0, T10:a0, T11:a0
- Row 3: T12:a0, T13:a0, T14:a0, T15:a0
- Row 4: T16:a0, T17:a0, T18:a0, T19:a0
- Row 5: T20:a0, T21:a0, T22:a0, T23:a0
- Row 6: T24:a0, T25:a0, T26:a0, T27:a0

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full row of matrix A across all 4 K-columns.
- Each row of matrix A is assigned to exactly 4 threads.
- 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31).

REGISTER PACKING:
- Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: a0
- Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each a0 fragment corresponds to one 64-bit double-precision value.
- Each thread holds one element of matrix A (one f64 value in register a0).
- No packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).

ADDRESSING NOTE:
- The figure label "%laneid:{fragments}" indicates that the lane ID (thread ID within the warp) directly determines which matrix element is held, following the pattern described above.
mma-884-B-f64.png:
MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k4
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: Matrix rows 0 through 3 (K dimension = 4, so rows 0-3)
   Columns: Matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8, so columns 0-7)
   The table header shows columns: 0, 1, 2, .. (implied 3-6), 7

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete layout)

   Row\Column  |  Col 0   |  Col 1   |  Col 2  |  ...  |  Col 7
   ------------|----------|----------|---------|-------|--------
   Row 0       |  T0:b0   |  T4:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T28:b0
   Row 1       |  T1:b0   |  T5:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T29:b0
   Row 2       |  T2:b0   |  T6:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T30:b0
   Row 3       |  T3:b0   |  T7:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T31:b0

4. DETAILED THREAD ASSIGNMENTS
   Column 0: T0:b0 (row 0), T1:b0 (row 1), T2:b0 (row 2), T3:b0 (row 3)
   Column 1: T4:b0 (row 0), T5:b0 (row 1), T6:b0 (row 2), T7:b0 (row 3)
   Column 2: T8:b0 (row 0), T9:b0 (row 1), T10:b0 (row 2), T11:b0 (row 3)  [implied by pattern]
   Column 3: T12:b0 (row 0), T13:b0 (row 1), T14:b0 (row 2), T15:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 4: T16:b0 (row 0), T17:b0 (row 1), T18:b0 (row 2), T19:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 5: T20:b0 (row 0), T21:b0 (row 1), T22:b0 (row 2), T23:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 6: T24:b0 (row 0), T25:b0 (row 1), T26:b0 (row 2), T27:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 7: T28:b0 (row 0), T29:b0 (row 1), T30:b0 (row 2), T31:b0 (row 3)

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers all 4 rows of a single column.
   Each group of 4 threads maps to one column of matrix B.
   There are 8 groups of 4 threads total, covering all 8 columns (N=8).
   Thread index within group determines the row (0-3).
   Group number (thread_id / 4) determines the column (0-7).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly one fragment register: b0
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value.
   No packing of multiple values per register (unlike f16 which packs two values).
   Each thread contributes one element of matrix B.

7. ADDRESSING FORMULA
   %laneid (lane ID within warp) maps as follows:
   column = laneid / 4  (integer division)
   row    = laneid % 4
   Register fragment: b0
mma-884-C-f64.png:
MMA Fragment Layout Description: .m8n8k4 Accumulator Matrix C/D with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Operation: mma .m8n8k4
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix logical dimensions: 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows in figure: matrix rows 0 through 7 (M dimension = 8)
   Columns in figure: matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8), shown as paired columns: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

3. COLUMN PAIRING
   Columns are grouped into 4 pairs:
   Pair 1: columns 0 and 1
   Pair 2: columns 2 and 3
   Pair 3: columns 4 and 5
   Pair 4: columns 6 and 7

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row and column pair)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (implied continuation, shown by arrow indicating pattern continues)

   Rows 3-6:
     (denoted by ".." with bidirectional arrows indicating the pattern continues in the same manner)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each row of the 8x8 matrix is handled by 4 threads.
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full matrix row across all 8 columns.
   Thread assignment per row:
     Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
     Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11   (implied)
     Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
   Within each row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns:
     Thread offset 0 within group: columns 0-1
     Thread offset 1 within group: columns 2-3
     Thread offset 2 within group: columns 4-5
     Thread offset 3 within group: columns 6-7

6. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 elements: c0 and c1.
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each element (c0, c1) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers as used in PTX for f64).
   c0 corresponds to the first column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   c1 corresponds to the second column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 2 f64 accumulator values covering a 1-row by 2-column sub-tile of the 8x8 output matrix.

7. LABEL NOTE
   The figure footer reads: %laneid:{fragments}
   This indicates the mapping is expressed as lane ID to fragment registers, where each lane (thread) within the warp holds the listed fragment registers {c0, c1}.
mma-8816-A-i8.png:
MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16
   Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: 0 through 7 (representing 8 rows of the matrix, mapped to groups of 4 threads each)
   Columns: 0 through 15 (representing the K=16 dimension)
   Column groupings: cols 0-3, cols 4-7, cols 8-11, cols 12-15
   Total threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads = one warp)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row)

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Cols 0-3:   T0  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T1  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T2  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T3  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Cols 0-3:   T4  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T5  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T6  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T7  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     Pattern continues (indicated by arrows showing continuation)
     Threads T8-T11 hold {a0, a1, a2, a3} across cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." with arrows showing the pattern continues identically
     Threads T12-T27 follow the same pattern in groups of 4

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Cols 0-3:   T28 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T29 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T30 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T31 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
   Each matrix row (0-7) is assigned to exactly 4 consecutive threads.
   Row 0 -> T0, T1, T2, T3
   Row 1 -> T4, T5, T6, T7
   Row 2 -> T8, T9, T10, T11
   Row 3 -> T12, T13, T14, T15
   Row 4 -> T16, T17, T18, T19
   Row 5 -> T20, T21, T22, T23
   Row 6 -> T24, T25, T26, T27
   Row 7 -> T28, T29, T30, T31
   Formula: thread = (matrix_row * 4) + (col_group), where col_group = 0,1,2,3 for cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 elements labeled a0, a1, a2, a3.
   Since each element is 8-bit (.u8 or .s8) and PTX registers are 32-bit, four 8-bit values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread uses 1 register (32-bit) containing 4 packed 8-bit values: a0 in bits 7:0, a1 in bits 15:8, a2 in bits 23:16, a3 in bits 31:24.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive K-dimension elements within its assigned column group.

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the mapping is from lane ID (thread index within warp) to the fragment elements held by that lane.
mma-8816-C-i8.png:
MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   - Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   - Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   - Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (0 through 7) represent the 8 rows of the accumulator matrix
   - Columns (0 through 7) represent the 8 columns of the accumulator matrix
   - Column pairs are grouped: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   - Threads T0 through T31 (32 threads total, one warp)
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 elements: {c0, c1}
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element occupies one full 32-bit register
   - Each thread therefore uses 2 registers for this fragment

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (Pattern continues with arrows indicating continuation from row 1 pattern downward)
     Threads T8-T11 follow the same column grouping pattern

   Rows 3-6:
     (Indicated by ".." — pattern continues consistently)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Each row of the 8x8 accumulator matrix is covered by 4 threads
   - Each of those 4 threads covers 2 consecutive columns (a column pair)
   - Row 0 → Threads T0, T1, T2, T3
   - Row 1 → Threads T4, T5, T6, T7
   - Row 2 → Threads T8, T9, T10, T11
   - Row 3 → Threads T12, T13, T14, T15
   - Row 4 → Threads T16, T17, T18, T19
   - Row 5 → Threads T20, T21, T22, T23
   - Row 6 → Threads T24, T25, T26, T27
   - Row 7 → Threads T28, T29, T30, T31
   - General rule: For matrix row R, threads T(4R) through T(4R+3) hold that row
   - Within each row, thread T(4R+0) holds columns 0-1, T(4R+1) holds columns 2-3, T(4R+2) holds columns 4-5, T(4R+3) holds columns 6-7

6. ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT WITHIN EACH THREAD
   - c0 corresponds to the lower-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 0 for T0, column 2 for T1)
   - c1 corresponds to the higher-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 1 for T0, column 3 for T1)
   - Each of c0 and c1 is one 32-bit .s32 register (no packing; one element per register)
   - Total registers per thread for this fragment: 2
mma-8832-A-i4.png:
MMA .m8n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u4/.s4 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix: A
Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit) or .s4 (signed 4-bit)
Total threads involved: 32 (T0 through T31)
Notation format: %laneid:{fragments}

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows representing matrix rows (0 through 7) and columns representing the K-dimension column ranges (0..7, 8..15, 16..23, 24..31). Each cell contains a thread ID and its associated register fragments.

COLUMN GROUPINGS (K-dimension segments)
Col 0..7    = K indices 0 through 7
Col 8..15   = K indices 8 through 15
Col 16..23  = K indices 16 through 23
Col 24..31  = K indices 24 through 31

FULL THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (Row by Row)

Row 0:
  Col 0..7:   T0  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 8..15:  T1  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 16..23: T2  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 24..31: T3  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

Row 1:
  Col 0..7:   T4  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 8..15:  T5  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 16..23: T6  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 24..31: T7  holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

Row 2:
  (implied continuation of pattern with arrow indicating progression)
  Threads T8, T9, T10, T11 (following the same pattern)

Rows 3..6:
  (implied by the ".." notation and arrows, continuing the pattern)

Row 7:
  Col 0..7:   T28 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 8..15:  T29 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 16..23: T30 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}
  Col 24..31: T31 holds {a0, a1, ..., a7}

GROUPING PATTERN
Each matrix row is handled by 4 consecutive threads (groups of 4).
Row 0: T0-T3
Row 1: T4-T7
Row 2: T8-T11
Row 3: T12-T15
Row 4: T16-T19
Row 5: T20-T23
Row 6: T24-T27
Row 7: T28-T31

Within each row, the 4 threads each cover 8 consecutive K-dimension columns:
Thread offset 0 in group: covers K cols 0..7
Thread offset 1 in group: covers K cols 8..15
Thread offset 2 in group: covers K cols 16..23
Thread offset 3 in group: covers K cols 24..31

REGISTER PACKING DETAILS
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled a0 through a7.
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 four-bit values pack into a single 32-bit register (8 x 4 bits = 32 bits).
Therefore, each thread stores its 8 elements (a0..a7) in exactly 1 x 32-bit register.
Elements a0..a7 represent the 8 K-dimension values assigned to that thread within its column segment.

SUMMARY TABLE
Row | Threads  | K-cols covered per thread (each thread covers 8 K-cols)
0   | T0..T3   | T0:K0-7, T1:K8-15, T2:K16-23, T3:K24-31
1   | T4..T7   | T4:K0-7, T5:K8-15, T6:K16-23, T7:K24-31
2   | T8..T11  | T8:K0-7, T9:K8-15, T10:K16-23, T11:K24-31
3   | T12..T15 | T12:K0-7, T13:K8-15, T14:K16-23, T15:K24-31
4   | T16..T19 | T16:K0-7, T17:K8-15, T18:K16-23, T19:K24-31
5   | T20..T23 | T20:K0-7, T21:K8-15, T22:K16-23, T23:K24-31
6   | T24..T27 | T24:K0-7, T25:K8-15, T26:K16-23, T27:K24-31
7   | T28..T31 | T28:K0-7, T29:K8-15, T30:K16-23, T31:K24-31
mma-8832-B-i4.png:
MMA .m8n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u4/.s4 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix: B
Operation: MMA .m8n8k32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit) or .s4 (signed 4-bit)
Fragment identifier: %laneid:{fragments}

MATRIX DIMENSIONS
M = 8, N = 8, K = 32
This is the B matrix fragment layout.

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has rows and columns defined as follows:
- Row axis (left side): represents groups of matrix rows, labeled by row ranges: 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31
- Column axis (top): labeled Col 0, 1, 2, .., 7
- Each cell contains a thread identifier and a register vector of elements b0 through b7

THREAD ASSIGNMENT TABLE

Row\Col | Col 0  | Col 1  | Col 2 | .. | Col 7
--------|--------|--------|-------|----|-------
Rows 0-7   | T0     | T4     | (continues pattern) | .. | T28
Rows 8-15  | T1     | T5     | (continues pattern) | .. | T29
Rows 16-23 | T2     | T6     | (continues pattern) | .. | T30
Rows 24-31 | T3     | T7     | (continues pattern) | .. | T31

FULL THREAD MAPPING (Col 0 through Col 7, 4 threads per column group):
Col 0: T0 (rows 0-7), T1 (rows 8-15), T2 (rows 16-23), T3 (rows 24-31)
Col 1: T4 (rows 0-7), T5 (rows 8-15), T6 (rows 16-23), T7 (rows 24-31)
Col 2: T8 (rows 0-7), T9 (rows 8-15), T10 (rows 16-23), T11 (rows 24-31)
Col 3: T12 (rows 0-7), T13 (rows 8-15), T14 (rows 16-23), T15 (rows 24-31)
Col 4: T16 (rows 0-7), T17 (rows 8-15), T18 (rows 16-23), T19 (rows 24-31)
Col 5: T20 (rows 0-7), T21 (rows 8-15), T22 (rows 16-23), T23 (rows 24-31)
Col 6: T24 (rows 0-7), T25 (rows 8-15), T26 (rows 16-23), T27 (rows 24-31)
Col 7: T28 (rows 0-7), T29 (rows 8-15), T30 (rows 16-23), T31 (rows 24-31)

REGISTER ELEMENTS PER THREAD
Each thread holds a fragment vector containing 8 elements: b0, b1, .., b6, b7
These are listed vertically as:
  b0
  b1
  ..
  b6
  b7

ELEMENT PACKING
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register.
8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total = 1 register (32-bit register holds 8 x 4-bit values).
Therefore, all 8 elements b0 through b7 are packed into a single 32-bit register per thread.

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
- 32 threads total (T0 through T31) cover the entire B matrix fragment.
- Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per column of the N dimension.
- Within each column group, the 4 threads cover the 4 row bands (rows 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31) of the K dimension.
- Each thread holds 8 consecutive 4-bit elements (b0-b7) packed into one 32-bit register, covering 8 rows within its assigned row band.
- The arrow in the figure indicates the continuation of the pattern from Col 2 through Col 6 (columns not explicitly shown follow the same pattern as Col 0, 1, and 7).
mma-88128-A.png:
MMA .m8n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .b1 type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k128
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary / boolean)
   Full matrix dimensions: 8 rows x 128 columns (M=8, N=8, K=128)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows (R): 0 through 7 — these represent the 8 rows of matrix A
   Columns (C): 0 through 127 — these represent the 128 columns of matrix A
   Column groupings: 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127 (four groups of 32 columns each)
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row and column group)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-31:   T0  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T1  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T2  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T3  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-31:   T4  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T5  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T6  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T7  holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

   Rows 2 through 6 (indicated by arrows showing continuation of the same pattern):
     Each row uses the next group of 4 threads in sequence, following the same 4-thread-per-row pattern.
     Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
     Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-31:   T28 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 32-63:  T29 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 64-95:  T30 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}
     Columns 96-127: T31 holds {a0, a1, .. a31}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each matrix row is covered by exactly 4 consecutive threads.
   Each thread covers exactly 32 consecutive columns of its assigned row.
   Thread assignment: Thread T(4*row + col_group) covers row R and column group col_group*32 to col_group*32+31.
   Specifically: T(4r + c) where r = row index (0-7), c = column group index (0-3).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds registers labeled a0 through a31 (32 register elements).
   Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 bits are packed into each 32-bit register.
   Each thread holds 32 bits total, covering 32 consecutive 1-bit elements from its assigned 32-column segment.
   The notation {a0, a1, .. a31} indicates 32 individual 1-bit values packed into what is effectively one 32-bit register (or equivalently, 32 bits packed across registers).

6. ADDRESSING NOTE
   The figure notes: %laneid:{fragments}
   This confirms that the lane ID (0-31 within the warp) directly indexes which thread holds which fragment, following the pattern described above.
mma-88128-B.png:
MMA .m8n8k128 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .b1 (1-bit) Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   - Instruction: MMA .m8n8k128
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .b1 (1-bit binary)
   - Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 128 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows in table: represent K-dimension rows of matrix B (0 through 127, grouped in sets of 32)
   - Columns in table: represent N-dimension columns (Col 0, 1, 2, .. 7), i.e., 8 columns total
   - Each cell contains one thread's register fragment for that row-group and column

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   Row group 0-31 (K rows 0 through 31):
     Col 0: T0  holds b0, b1, .., b31  (32 elements, one per register or packed)
     Col 1: T4  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied, arrow indicates continuation pattern)
     ...
     Col 7: T28 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 32-63 (K rows 32 through 63):
     Col 0: T1  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T5  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Col 7: T29 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 64-95 (K rows 64 through 95):
     Col 0: T2  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T6  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Col 7: T30 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 96-127 (K rows 96 through 127):
     Col 0: T3  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T7  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Col 7: T31 holds b0, b1, .., b31

4. THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN (SUMMARY)
   - 32 threads total (T0 through T31) cover the full 128 x 8 matrix B fragment
   - The 128 K-rows are divided into 4 groups of 32 rows each
   - The 8 N-columns are divided across 8 thread slots per row group
   - Thread index formula: thread = (row_group) + (col * 4)
     where row_group = floor(K_row / 32) in {0,1,2,3} and col in {0..7}
     i.e., T(row_group + col*4)
   - Specifically:
     Col 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (for row groups 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127)
     Col 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Col 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
     Col 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
     Col 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
     Col 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
     Col 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
     Col 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds elements labeled b0 through b31 (32 elements)
   - Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 one-bit values pack into a single 32-bit register
   - Therefore each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 32 packed 1-bit values
   - The 32 bits correspond to 32 consecutive K-rows within the thread's assigned row group (one of the four 32-row bands) for a single N-column

6. ANNOTATION
   - The arrow in column 2 spanning from the top row group down to the bottom row group indicates the column-index direction continues similarly for all intermediate columns (2 through 6, shown as "..")
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragment} indicating the lane ID determines which fragment a thread holds
mma-88128-C.png:
MMA .m8n8k128 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   - Instruction: mma .m8n8k128
   - Matrix: C/D (Accumulator)
   - Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=128

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (labeled Row\Col): 0, 1, 2, .., 7  (8 rows total, representing matrix rows 0-7)
   - Columns: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7  (8 columns total, grouped into pairs: [0,1], [2,3], [4,5], [6,7])
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row)

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     (Indicated by arrow spanning the row — same pattern continues with threads T8-T11, implied by the arrow showing continuation of the pattern)

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." — pattern continues with threads T12-T27 following the same structure

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - 4 threads per matrix row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns
   - 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31)
   - Thread assignment per row:
       Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
       Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
       Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11  (implied)
       Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
       Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
       Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
       Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
       Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
   - Each thread Tn in row r covers columns [2*(n mod 4)*2 .. 2*(n mod 4)*2+1]

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 accumulator elements: c0 and c1
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element is one full 32-bit register
   - Therefore each thread holds 2 separate 32-bit registers: one for c0, one for c1
   - No sub-register packing (unlike f16 which packs two values per register)
   - c0 corresponds to the first (lower-index) column of the thread's column pair
   - c1 corresponds to the second (higher-index) column of the thread's column pair

6. SUMMARY TABLE
   Thread | Matrix Row | Matrix Cols | Registers
   T0     | 0          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T1     | 0          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T2     | 0          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T3     | 0          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   T4     | 1          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T5     | 1          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T6     | 1          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T7     | 1          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
   ...    | ...        | ...         | ...
   T28    | 7          | 0, 1        | c0, c1
   T29    | 7          | 2, 3        | c0, c1
   T30    | 7          | 4, 5        | c0, c1
   T31    | 7          | 6, 7        | c0, c1
mma-1684-A.png:
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k4
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix A logical dimensions: 16 rows x 4 columns (M=16, K=4)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row index (0-15) and columns labeled 0-3 (Col 0 through Col 3).
   Columns 0-3 represent the 4 column positions (K=4 dimension) of matrix A.
   The notation is: Tx:ay where x = thread lane ID, y = register fragment index (a0 or a1).

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete, per row)

   UPPER HALF (matrix rows 0-7, register fragment a0):

   Row 0:  Col0=T0:a0,  Col1=T1:a0,  Col2=T2:a0,  Col3=T3:a0
   Row 1:  Col0=T4:a0,  Col1=T5:a0,  Col2=T6:a0,  Col3=T7:a0
   Row 2:  (pattern continues with arrows indicating same thread/register assignment pattern)
   Rows 2-6: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 2-6 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 7:  Col0=T28:a0, Col1=T29:a0, Col2=T30:a0, Col3=T31:a0

   LOWER HALF (matrix rows 8-15, register fragment a1):

   Row 8:  Col0=T0:a1,  Col1=T1:a1,  Col2=T2:a1,  Col3=T3:a1
   Row 9:  Col0=T4:a1,  Col1=T5:a1,  Col2=T6:a1,  Col3=T7:a1
   Rows 10-14: implied continuation of the same pattern (T8-T27 covering rows 10-14 in groups of 4 threads per row)
   Row 15: Col0=T28:a1, Col1=T29:a1, Col2=T30:a1, Col3=T31:a1

4. DATA TYPE
   .f64 — each element is a 64-bit double precision float.
   Each element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX representation).

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds exactly 2 fragments: a0 and a1.
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per matrix row:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 0  (fragment a0)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 1  (fragment a0)
     T8-T11  -> matrix row 2  (fragment a0)
     T12-T15 -> matrix row 3  (fragment a0)
     T16-T19 -> matrix row 4  (fragment a0)
     T20-T23 -> matrix row 5  (fragment a0)
     T24-T27 -> matrix row 6  (fragment a0)
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 7  (fragment a0)
   The same thread grouping repeats for rows 8-15 using fragment a1:
     T0-T3   -> matrix row 8  (fragment a1)
     T4-T7   -> matrix row 9  (fragment a1)
     ...
     T28-T31 -> matrix row 15 (fragment a1)
   Within each group of 4 threads, each thread holds one of the 4 column elements (columns 0-3).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each matrix element is stored in one dedicated 64-bit register fragment.
   There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).
   Each thread holds 2 registers total for matrix A: a0 (one f64 element from the upper 8 rows) and a1 (one f64 element from the lower 8 rows).

   Index label: %laneid:{fragments} — the lane ID determines which column and row the thread's element corresponds to.
mma-1684-B.png:
MMA .m16n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX IDENTIFICATION
   - Instruction: mma.m16n8k4
   - Matrix: B (input matrix)
   - Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   - Matrix B dimensions in this operation: K x N = 4 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 3), total of 4 rows (K=4 dimension)
   - Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7), total of 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   - The table header shows: Row\Column | 0 | 1 | 2 | .. | 7
   - Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the ".." notation with an arrow indicating continuation

3. THREAD AND REGISTER ASSIGNMENT (complete mapping)

   Row 0, Column 0: Thread T0,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 1: Thread T4,  register b0
   Row 0, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 0, Column 7: Thread T28, register b0

   Row 1, Column 0: Thread T1,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 1: Thread T5,  register b0
   Row 1, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 1, Column 7: Thread T29, register b0

   Row 2, Column 0: Thread T2,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 1: Thread T6,  register b0
   Row 2, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 2, Column 7: Thread T30, register b0

   Row 3, Column 0: Thread T3,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 1: Thread T7,  register b0
   Row 3, Column 2: (implied continuation)
   Row 3, Column 7: Thread T31, register b0

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Pattern continues: threads T(4*c) through T(4*c+3) cover column c, rows 0-3
   - Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3 respectively, each holding b0
   - Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) maps to one column of matrix B
   - Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads, one full warp)

5. REGISTER USAGE PER THREAD
   - Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: b0
   - Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value
   - Each thread stores exactly one matrix element of matrix B

6. REGISTER PACKING
   - Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision)
   - One element per register (no packing of multiple values into a single register, unlike f16 which packs two values)
   - Each thread holds 1 register (b0) containing 1 f64 element

7. NOTATION
   - Format in figure: T{laneid}:b{register_index}
   - %laneid:{fragments} label at bottom confirms lane ID determines fragment assignment
   - The arrow in columns 2-6 area indicates the pattern continues uniformly across all intermediate columns
mma-1688-A-f16.png:
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: MMA .m16n8k8
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
MATRIX DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=8

GRID STRUCTURE
--------------
The table has Row\Col headers.
Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 15).
Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7).
Only columns 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 are shown, but thread assignments only appear in columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 (pairs).

Specifically, thread assignments appear at column positions 0, 2, 4, 6 (each spanning two columns visually), corresponding to 4 thread groups per row.

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
--------------------------

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 0 through 7):

Row 0:  col 0-1: T0:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T1:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T2:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T3:{a0, a1}
Row 1:  col 0-1: T4:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T5:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T6:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T7:{a0, a1}
Row 2:  (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 7:  col 0-1: T28:{a0, a1}  col 2-3: T29:{a0, a1}  col 4-5: T30:{a0, a1}  col 6-7: T31:{a0, a1}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 8 through 15):

Row 8:  col 0-1: T0:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T1:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T2:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T3:{a2, a3}
Row 9:  col 0-1: T4:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T5:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T6:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T7:{a2, a3}
Row 10: (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 15: col 0-1: T28:{a2, a3}  col 2-3: T29:{a2, a3}  col 4-5: T30:{a2, a3}  col 6-7: T31:{a2, a3}

GROUPING PATTERN
----------------
Rows 0-7 map to threads T0 through T31 (one thread per pair of rows, 4 threads per row across columns 0-7).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns.
Rows 0-1: T0-T7 (T0,T1,T2,T3 on row 0; T4,T5,T6,T7 on row 1)
Rows 2-3: T8-T15 (implied by arrows)
Rows 4-5: T16-T23 (implied by arrows)
Rows 6-7: T24-T31 (T28,T29,T30,T31 on row 7 explicitly shown)

The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15, but with different register elements {a2, a3} instead of {a0, a1}.

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the upper half (rows 0-7): a0 and a1, packed as {a0, a1} in one 32-bit register (two f16 or bf16 values packed into a single 32-bit register).
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the lower half (rows 8-15): a2 and a3, packed as {a2, a3} in one 32-bit register.
Total per thread: 4 elements (a0, a1, a2, a3) stored in 2 registers of 32 bits each.

LABEL AT BOTTOM
---------------
%laneid:{fragments}
This indicates the notation used: lane ID maps to the listed fragment elements.

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   UPPER-LEFT QUADRANT (rows 0-7, columns 0-3): register element a0
     Row 0:  col 0 = T0:a0,  col 1 = T1:a0,  col 2 = T2:a0,  col 3 = T3:a0

     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (rows 8-15, columns 4-7): register element a3
     Row 8:  col 4 = T0:a3,  col 5 = T1:a3,  col 6 = T2:a3,  col 7 = T3:a3
     Row 9:  col 4 = T4:a3,  col 5 = T5:a3,  col 6 = T6:a3,  col 7 = T7:a3
     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 15: col 4 = T28:a3, col 5 = T29:a3, col 6 = T30:a3, col 7 = T31:a3

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:
     Threads T0-T3   cover row 0 (and rows 0,8 for a0/a1/a2/a3)
     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)
     Threads T8-T11  cover rows 2,10 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T12-T15 cover rows 3,11 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T16-T19 cover rows 4,12 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T20-T23 cover rows 5,13 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T24-T27 cover rows 6,14 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T28-T31 cover row 7 (and rows 7,15)
   Each group of 4 threads covers one matrix row across 4 columns in each half.

   Each thread Tn holds exactly 4 register elements:
     a0: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread

mma-1688-B-f16.png:
MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f16/.bf16 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=8
   Data types: .f16 or .bf16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table shows:
   Rows (labeled "Row\Column"): represent pairs of matrix rows (0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7), covering 8 rows total (K=8 dimension)
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .. , 7 — representing 8 columns (N=8 dimension)
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   Row pair 0-1 (matrix rows 0 and 1):
     Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern, arrow indicates continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 2-3 (matrix rows 2 and 3):
     Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 4-5 (matrix rows 4 and 5):
     Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T30 holds {b0, b1}

   Row pair 6-7 (matrix rows 6 and 7):
     Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1}
     Column 2: (continues pattern)
     ...
     Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1}

4. DATA TYPE
   .f16 or .bf16 (both supported as indicated in the caption)

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped in blocks of 4 consecutive threads per row-pair group:
     T0-T3:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, all at column 0
     T4-T7:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 1
     T8-T11:  column 2 (implied by arrow continuation)
     T12-T15: column 3
     T16-T19: column 4
     T20-T23: column 5
     T24-T27: column 6
     T28-T31: cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 7

   General rule: Thread T(n) covers:
     Row pair = n mod 4 (i.e., row pair index 0,1,2,3 corresponding to rows 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7)
     Column = floor(n / 4)

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly 2 register elements: b0 and b1
   These are packed as two f16 (or bf16) values within a single 32-bit register
   b0 occupies the lower 16 bits of the register
   b1 occupies the upper 16 bits of the register
   Each thread thus holds one 32-bit register containing two consecutive elements from its assigned matrix position

   The notation %laneid:{fragments} at the bottom confirms the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:

     Upper section: rows 0-3 (K rows 0 through 3)
     Lower section: rows 4-7 (K rows 4 through 7)

3. ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   Upper section (rows 0-3), register b0:
     Row 0, Column 0: T0:b0
     Row 0, Column 1: T4:b0
     Row 0, Column 7: T28:b0
     Row 1, Column 0: T1:b0
     Row 1, Column 1: T5:b0
     Row 1, Column 7: T29:b0

     Row 2, Column 1: T6:b0
     Row 2, Column 7: T30:b0
     Row 3, Column 0: T3:b0
     Row 3, Column 1: T7:b0
     Row 3, Column 7: T31:b0

   Lower section (rows 4-7), register b1:
     Row 4, Column 0: T0:b1
     Row 4, Column 1: T4:b1
     Row 4, Column 7: T28:b1
     Row 5, Column 0: T1:b1
     Row 5, Column 1: T5:b1
     Row 5, Column 7: T29:b1
     Row 6, Column 0: T2:b1
     Row 6, Column 1: T6:b1
     Row 6, Column 7: T30:b1
     Row 7, Column 0: T3:b1
     Row 7, Column 1: T7:b1
     Row 7, Column 7: T31:b1

   Columns 2 through 6 (indicated by ".." with diagonal arrows) follow the same pattern as shown for columns 0, 1, and 7, continuing the thread numbering pattern.

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per column group:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)

   Each group of 4 threads (Tn, Tn+1, Tn+2, Tn+3) covers one column of matrix B across all 8 K-rows, using registers b0 (for K-rows 0-3) and b1 (for K-rows 4-7).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 registers for matrix B:
     b0: holds the element from K-rows 0-3 for that thread's assigned column
     b1: holds the element from K-rows 4-7 for that thread's assigned column
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each register b0 and b1 is a 64-bit register holding one double-precision value. There is no sub-register packing (one element per register).

6. NOTATION
   %laneid:{fragments} indicates that the thread lane ID determines which fragment (matrix element) each thread holds.
   The diagonal arrows in columns 2 and ".." indicate the pattern continues consistently for all intermediate columns following the same thread-numbering scheme.
mma-16816-A-f16.png:
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: mma .m16n8k16
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=16

GRID INTERPRETATION
-------------------
Rows (R) = matrix rows 0 through 15 (the M=16 dimension)
Columns (C) = matrix columns 0 through 15 (the K=16 dimension)
The table is divided into two halves:
  Left half: columns 0-7
  Right half: columns 8-15

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Two f16 (or bf16) values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
Each thread holds 4 registers total: {a0,a1}, {a2,a3}, {a4,a5}, {a6,a7}
- {a0,a1} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a0 and a1)
- {a2,a3} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a2 and a3)
- {a4,a5} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a4 and a5)
- {a6,a7} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a6 and a7)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete table)
------------------------------------------
Format: Row, Column range -> Thread: {fragments}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 0-3: T0:{a0,a1}  T1:{a0,a1}  T2:{a0,a1}  T3:{a0,a1}
  Row 1, Cols 0-3: T4:{a0,a1}  T5:{a0,a1}  T6:{a0,a1}  T7:{a0,a1}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern repeating)
  Row 7, Cols 0-3: T28:{a0,a1} T29:{a0,a1} T30:{a0,a1} T31:{a0,a1}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 8-11:  T0:{a4,a5}  T1:{a4,a5}  T2:{a4,a5}  T3:{a4,a5}
  Row 1, Cols 8-11:  T4:{a4,a5}  T5:{a4,a5}  T6:{a4,a5}  T7:{a4,a5}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern
  Row 7, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a4,a5} T29:{a4,a5} T30:{a4,a5} T31:{a4,a5}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 0-3: T0:{a2,a3}  T1:{a2,a3}  T2:{a2,a3}  T3:{a2,a3}
  Row 9,  Cols 0-3: T4:{a2,a3}  T5:{a2,a3}  T6:{a2,a3}  T7:{a2,a3}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 0-3: T28:{a2,a3} T29:{a2,a3} T30:{a2,a3} T31:{a2,a3}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 8-11:  T0:{a6,a7}  T1:{a6,a7}  T2:{a6,a7}  T3:{a6,a7}
  Row 9,  Cols 8-11:  T4:{a6,a7}  T5:{a6,a7}  T6:{a6,a7}  T7:{a6,a7}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a6,a7} T29:{a6,a7} T30:{a6,a7} T31:{a6,a7}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
-------------------------
Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads, each group covering one matrix row:
  Row 0:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 1:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 2:  T8,  T9,  T10, T11  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 3:  T12, T13, T14, T15  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 4:  T16, T17, T18, T19  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 5:  T20, T21, T22, T23  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 6:  T24, T25, T26, T27  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 7:  T28, T29, T30, T31  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 8:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11) -- same threads as row 0, different registers
  Row 9:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7
  ...
  Row 15: T28, T29, T30, T31

REGISTER ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD (all 32 threads):
  Each thread holds exactly 4 registers (8 f16 elements total):
  - Register containing {a0,a1}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a2,a3}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a4,a5}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 8-15
  - Register containing {a6,a7}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 8-15

NOTE: The column coverage per thread within each row spans 4 consecutive columns (e.g., cols 0-3 for T0-T3 in row 0). The exact column offset within the 4-column span is determined by the thread's position within its group of 4. Each thread covers exactly 1 column position in each of the four 4-column sub-blocks (cols 0-3, cols 4-7 implied by pattern, cols 8-11, cols 12-15 implied by pattern).
mma-16816-A-f64.png:
MMA .m16n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix: A
Instruction: mma.m16n8k16
Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
Matrix A dimensions: 16 rows x 16 columns (the K=16 dimension)
Total threads involved: 32 (T0 through T31)
Each thread holds 8 fragments: a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7

GRID STRUCTURE
The table uses R\C notation where:
- R (rows) = matrix rows (0-15)
- C (columns) = matrix columns (0-15), grouped into 4 sections of 4 columns each: [0-3], [4-7], [8-11], [12-15]

The 32 threads are divided into groups of 4 consecutive threads per cell. Each group of 4 threads (e.g., T0-T3) covers 4 consecutive columns in a given row.

COLUMN GROUPINGS AND FRAGMENT ASSIGNMENTS

Columns 0-3: fragments a0 (rows 0-7) and a1 (rows 8-15)
Columns 4-7: fragments a2 (rows 0-7) and a3 (rows 8-15)
Columns 8-11: fragments a4 (rows 0-7) and a5 (rows 8-15)
Columns 12-15: fragments a6 (rows 0-7) and a7 (rows 8-15)

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

ROW BAND 0-7 (upper half of matrix A):

Row 0, Cols 0-3:   T0:a0,  T1:a0,  T2:a0,  T3:a0
Row 0, Cols 4-7:   T0:a2,  T1:a2,  T2:a2,  T3:a2
Row 0, Cols 8-11:  T0:a4,  T1:a4,  T2:a4,  T3:a4
Row 0, Cols 12-15: T0:a6,  T1:a6,  T2:a6,  T3:a6

Row 1, Cols 0-3:   T4:a0,  T5:a0,  T6:a0,  T7:a0
Row 1, Cols 4-7:   T4:a2,  T5:a2,  T6:a2,  T7:a2
Row 1, Cols 8-11:  T4:a4,  T5:a4,  T6:a4,  T7:a4
Row 1, Cols 12-15: T4:a6,  T5:a6,  T6:a6,  T7:a6

Rows 2-6: continue the pattern (indicated by arrows in figure), incrementing thread group by 4 per row:
Row 2: T8-T11
Row 3: T12-T15
Row 4: T16-T19
Row 5: T20-T23
Row 6: T24-T27

Row 7, Cols 0-3:   T28:a0, T29:a0, T30:a0, T31:a0
Row 7, Cols 4-7:   T28:a2, T29:a2, T30:a2, T31:a2
Row 7, Cols 8-11:  T28:a4, T29:a4, T30:a4, T31:a4
Row 7, Cols 12-15: T28:a6, T29:a6, T30:a6, T31:a6

ROW BAND 8-15 (lower half of matrix A):

Row 8, Cols 0-3:   T0:a1,  T1:a1,  T2:a1,  T3:a1
Row 8, Cols 4-7:   T0:a3,  T1:a3,  T2:a3,  T3:a3
Row 8, Cols 8-11:  T0:a5,  T1:a5,  T2:a5,  T3:a5
Row 8, Cols 12-15: T0:a7,  T1:a7,  T2:a7,  T3:a7

Row 9, Cols 0-3:   T4:a1,  T5:a1,  T6:a1,  T7:a1
Row 9, Cols 4-7:   T4:a3,  T5:a3,  T6:a3,  T7:a3
Row 9, Cols 8-11:  T4:a5,  T5:a5,  T6:a5,  T7:a5
Row 9, Cols 12-15: T4:a7,  T5:a7,  T6:a7,  T7:a7

Rows 10-14: continue the pattern (indicated by arrows in figure), incrementing thread group by 4 per row:
Row 10: T8-T11
Row 11: T12-T15
Row 12: T16-T19
Row 13: T20-T23
Row 14: T24-T27

Row 15, Cols 0-3:   T28:a1, T29:a1, T30:a1, T31:a1
Row 15, Cols 4-7:   T28:a3, T29:a3, T30:a3, T31:a3
Row 15, Cols 8-11:  T28:a5, T29:a5, T30:a5, T31:a5
Row 15, Cols 12-15: T28:a7, T29:a7, T30:a7, T31:a7

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY

Each thread Tn holds exactly 8 fragments: a0 through a7.
Thread group assignment per row: row r uses threads T(4r) through T(4r+3) for r=0..7 in both the upper (rows 0-7) and lower (rows 8-15) bands.

Thread Tn (where n = 4r + c, r=0..7, c=0..3):
- a0: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a1: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a2: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a3: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a4: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+8,  cols 8-11 band
- a5: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+8,  cols 8-11 band
- a6: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+12, cols 12-15 band
- a7: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+12, cols 12-15 band

REGISTER PACKING
Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each fragment (a0 through a7) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX). There is no packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register). Each fragment corresponds to exactly one double-precision scalar value.

LABEL FORMAT
The figure uses the notation: laneid:{fragments}, meaning each cell entry is formatted as T[laneid]:a[fragment_index].

Row 8:  Col 0 = T0:B2,  Col 1 = T4:B2,  Col 7 = T28:B2
Row 9:  Col 0 = T1:B2,  Col 1 = T5:B2,  Col 7 = T29:B2
Row 10: Col 0 = T2:B2,  Col 1 = T6:B2,  Col 7 = T30:B2
Row 11: Col 0 = T3:B2,  Col 1 = T7:B2,  Col 7 = T31:B2

ROWS 12-15 (fourth group, register B3):
Row 12: Col 0 = T0:B3,  Col 1 = T4:B3,  Col 7 = T28:B3
Row 13: Col 0 = T1:B3,  Col 1 = T5:B3,  Col 7 = T29:B3
Row 14: Col 0 = T2:B3,  Col 1 = T6:B3,  Col 7 = T30:B3
Row 15: Col 0 = T3:B3,  Col 1 = T7:B3,  Col 7 = T31:B3

4. DATA TYPE
.f64 = 64-bit double precision floating point
Each register fragment (B0, B1, B2, B3) holds one f64 value (one 64-bit element), stored as a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX (since PTX registers are 32-bit, each f64 occupies two consecutive 32-bit registers).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS

Column grouping (N dimension, 8 columns total):
Columns 0-7 are distributed across threads T0-T31 in groups of 4 threads per column-pair.
Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of matrix B.
Specifically:
  Column 0: threads T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0,1,2,3 respectively within each row-group)
  Column 1: threads T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: threads T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: threads T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: threads T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: threads T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: threads T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: threads T28, T29, T30, T31

Row grouping (K dimension, 16 rows total):
Rows 0-3   map to register B0 for all threads
Rows 4-7   map to register B1 for all threads
Rows 8-11  map to register B2 for all threads
Rows 12-15 map to register B3 for all threads

Each thread holds 4 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3.

6. REGISTER PACKING
Data type is .f64 (64-bit). In PTX, f64 values require 64 bits of storage.
Each fragment Bn corresponds to one f64 element, stored in a 64-bit register (or equivalently two 32-bit registers as a register pair in PTX notation).
There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 where two values share one 32-bit register). Each f64 element occupies its own 64-bit register fragment.
Each thread holds exactly 4 f64 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3, covering one column of matrix B across all 16 K-rows.

SUMMARY PER THREAD:
Every thread Tx (where x = 0 to 31) holds:
  B0: element at matrix B[row_group_0][col_x] where col_x = floor(x/4), row within group = x mod 4, rows 0-3
  B1: element at matrix B[row_group_1][col_x], rows 4-7
  B2: element at matrix B[row_group_2][col_x], rows 8-11
  B3: element at matrix B[row_group_3][col_x], rows 12-15

The label at the bottom "%laneid:{fragments}" confirms that the lane ID (thread index within warp, 0-31) determines which column and which row-within-group each thread owns.
mma-16816-B-i8.png:
MMA .m16n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k16
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:

   Columns: labeled 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (representing 8 columns of matrix B, i.e., N=8)
   Threads shown: T0 through T31

3. ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each thread holds 4 elements packed into registers: b0, b1, b2, b3
   Since .u8/.s8 are 8-bit values, four 8-bit values are packed into a single 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one 32-bit register).
   The notation shows a curly brace grouping {b0, b1, b2, b3} per thread per column group.

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (Row groups and Column assignments)

   ROWS 0-3 (matrix rows 0, 1, 2, 3):
     Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 2: (indicated by arrow continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   ROWS 4-7 (matrix rows 4, 5, 6, 7):
     Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   ROWS 8-11 (matrix rows 8, 9, 10, 11):
     Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   ROWS 12-15 (matrix rows 12, 13, 14, 15):
     Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
     Column 2: (continuation)
     ...
     Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

5. GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
   Thread numbering follows this pattern:
   - Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
   - Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
   - This pattern continues: threads T(4c), T(4c+1), T(4c+2), T(4c+3) cover column c for c=0..7
   - Threads T28, T29, T30, T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
   - Total threads used: 32 (T0-T31), each covering one column-group-of-4-rows block

6. REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS
   Each thread stores 4 consecutive 8-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3) packed into one 32-bit register.
   b0 occupies bits [7:0], b1 bits [15:8], b2 bits [23:16], b3 bits [31:24] (standard byte packing).
   The 4 elements correspond to 4 consecutive rows within the assigned row-group for that thread.

7. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragment} — indicating the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp to the fragment register.
mma-16832-A-i8.png:
MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix A
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

==============================================================
1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
==============================================================
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=32
This figure shows how the 16 rows x 32 columns of matrix A are distributed across 32 threads (T0-T31) in a warp.

==============================================================
2. GRID STRUCTURE
==============================================================
The figure is organized as a 2D grid:
- Rows (labeled R\C on left): Matrix rows 0-15, grouped into two bands: rows 0-7 (top half) and rows 8-15 (bottom half)
- Columns: Matrix columns 0-31, split into two halves: columns 0-15 (left panel) and columns 16-31 (right panel)
- Column groupings shown: 0..4, 5..7, 8..11, 12..15 (left panel) and 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31 (right panel)
- Each cell shows which thread holds which register elements for that row/column range

The lane ID annotation at the bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

==============================================================
3. ELEMENT NAMING AND REGISTER PACKING
==============================================================
Elements are named a0 through a15. Multiple elements are packed into each register.
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) or sub-byte float types, four elements fit per 32-bit register.
Each thread holds 4 registers total (covering a0..a3, a4..a7, a8..a11, a12..a15).

Register groupings per thread:
- Register group 1: {a0, a1, .., a3}   (elements a0, a1, a2, a3 packed into one 32-bit register)
- Register group 2: {a4, .., a7}        (elements a4, a5, a6, a7 packed into one 32-bit register)
- Register group 3: {a8, .., a11}       (elements a8, a9, a10, a11 packed into one 32-bit register)
- Register group 4: {a12, .., a15}      (elements a12, a13, a14, a15 packed into one 32-bit register)

==============================================================
4. DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
==============================================================

TOP-LEFT PANEL: Matrix rows 0-7, columns 0-15
--------------------------------------------------------------
Row 0:  T0:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T1:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T2:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T3:{a0,a1,..,a3}
Row 1:  T4:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T5:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T6:{a0,a1,..,a3}   T7:{a0,a1,..,a3}
Row 2:  (arrow indicating continuation pattern downward/rightward)
...:    (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
Row 7:  T28:{a0,a1,..,a3}  T29:{a0,a1,..,a3}  T30:{a0,a1,..,a3}  T31:{a0,a1,..,a3}

TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Matrix rows 0-7, columns 16-31
--------------------------------------------------------------
Row 0:  T0:{a8,..,a11}     T1:{a8,..,a11}     T2:{a8,..,a11}     T3:{a8,..,a11}
Row 1:  T4:{a8,..,a11}     T5:{a8,..,a11}     T6:{a8,..,a11}     T7:{a8,..,a11}
Row 2:  (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
...:    (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
Row 7:  T28:{a8,..,a11}    T29:{a8,..,a11}    T30:{a8,..,a11}    T31:{a8,..,a11}

BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Matrix rows 8-15, columns 0-15
--------------------------------------------------------------
Row 8:  T0:{a4,..,a7}      T1:{a4,..,a7}      T2:{a4,..,a7}      T3:{a4,..,a7}
Row 9:  T4:{a4,..,a7}      T5:{a4,..,a7}      T6:{a4,..,a7}      T7:{a4,..,a7}
Row 10: (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
...:    (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
Row 15: T28:{a4,..,a7}     T29:{a4,..,a7}     T30:{a4,..,a7}     T31:{a4,..,a7}

BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Matrix rows 8-15, columns 16-31
--------------------------------------------------------------
Row 8:  T0:{a12,..,a15}    T1:{a12,..,a15}    T2:{a12,..,a15}    T3:{a12,..,a15}
Row 9:  T4:{a12,..,a15}    T5:{a12,..,a15}    T6:{a12,..,a15}    T7:{a12,..,a15}
Row 10: (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
...:    (arrow indicating continuation pattern)
Row 15: T28:{a12,..,a15}   T29:{a12,..,a15}   T30:{a12,..,a15}   T31:{a12,..,a15}

==============================================================
5. GROUPING PATTERNS SUMMARY
==============================================================
- All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
- Threads T0-T3 cover matrix row 0 (columns 0-15 via a0..a3, columns 16-31 via a8..a11).
- Threads T4-T7 cover matrix row 1 (same element assignments).
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one matrix row.
- Rows 0-7 use register groups {a0..a3} (cols 0-15) and {a8..a11} (cols 16-31).
- Rows 8-15 use register groups {a4..a7} (cols 0-15) and {a12..a15} (cols 16-31).
- Within each row, all 4 threads in the group hold the SAME element indices (same a-values), meaning each thread holds the same elements — the column dimension within a row is replicated across the 4 threads in the group (the column index within the K dimension is determined by the thread's position within the warp group of 4).

==============================================================
6. REGISTER PACKING DETAIL
==============================================================
Each 32-bit register holds 4 elements of 8-bit type (u8 or s8).
For sub-byte float types (e4m3, e5m2, e3m2, e2m3, e2m1), packing follows the same 4-per-register pattern within 32 bits.
Each thread holds exactly 4 registers for matrix A in this m16n8k32 instruction:
  Register 0: a0, a1, a2, a3
  Register 1: a4, a5, a6, a7
  Register 2: a8, a9, a10, a11
  Register 3: a12, a13, a14, a15

MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u4/.s4 Type

OVERVIEW
Matrix: B
Operation: MMA m16n8k32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer) or .s4 (signed 4-bit integer)
Matrix B dimensions: K x N = 32 rows x 8 columns

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
- Rows (vertical axis): represent matrix row ranges (0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31), corresponding to K dimension
- Columns (horizontal axis): represent matrix column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (N dimension, 8 columns total)
- Columns 0 and 1 are explicitly shown; column 2 and columns up to 7 are indicated with ".." notation
- Column 7 is explicitly shown

THREAD ASSIGNMENT BY ROW BAND AND COLUMN

Row band 0-7 (K rows 0 through 7):
  Column 0: Thread T0, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T4, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T28, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 8-15 (K rows 8 through 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 16-23 (K rows 16 through 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 24-31 (K rows 24 through 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0, rows 0-7 / 8-15 / 16-23 / 24-31 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, same row band pattern
- Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next column
- Thread Tx for column c, row band r: T = c*4 + r (where r = 0,1,2,3 for row bands 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
- Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled b0 through b7 within a single register (shown as a column vector).
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total, fitting exactly into one 32-bit register.
So each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 8 packed 4-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7).

ELEMENT COVERAGE PER THREAD
Each thread covers 8 consecutive K-rows within its assigned row band and one specific N-column.
The 8 elements b0-b7 correspond to the 8 matrix rows within that thread's row band for its assigned column.

ARROW NOTATION
An arrow is drawn from the cell at row band 0-7 / column 2 area down to row band 24-31 / column 2 area, indicating the ".." columns follow the same pattern as the explicitly shown columns.

SUMMARY TABLE
Row Band  | Col 0 | Col 1 | Col 2 | ... | Col 7
0-7       |  T0   |  T4   |  T8   | ... |  T28
8-15      |  T1   |  T5   |  T9   | ... |  T29
16-23     |  T2   |  T6   |  T10  | ... |  T30
24-31     |  T3   |  T7   |  T11  | ... |  T31

Each thread holds 1 register with 8 packed 4-bit values (b0-b7), totaling 32 bits per register.

Matrix: B (input matrix)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (of the table) represent groups of matrix rows
- Columns (of the table) represent column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7

TABLE LAYOUT (Row \ Col):

Row group 0..3, Col 0: T0:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row group 0..3, Col 2: (implied continuation)

Row group 4..7, Col 0: T1:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 4..7, Col 1: T5:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 4..7, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 4..7, Col 7: T29: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row group 8..11, Col 0: T2:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 8..11, Col 1: T6:  {b0, b1, b2, b3}
Row group 8..11, Col 2: (implied continuation)
Row group 8..11, Col 7: T30: {b0, b1, b2, b3}

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN:

- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column group 1
- Threads T8-T11 cover the same row groups at column group 2 (implied)
- Threads T28-T31 cover the same row groups at column group 7

General formula:
- Thread index = (col_group * 4) + row_group
  where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15
  and col_group = 0..7

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 4 elements: b0, b1, b2, b3
These 4 elements are packed into registers as a group (shown as a single brace notation {b0, b1, b2, b3}).
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) or sub-byte types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register. Specifically, 4 elements of 8-bit type fit into one 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one register). For sub-byte types, even more elements fit per register.

SUMMARY:
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) cover the full B fragment
- Each thread holds one register containing 4 packed elements (b0-b3)
- Column dimension (N=8, cols 0-7) is distributed across groups of 4 threads (T0,T4,T8,...,T28 for row group 0; T1,T5,T9,...,T29 for row group 1; etc.)
- Row dimension (rows 0-15, grouped into 4 groups of 4 rows) is distributed across threads within each group of 4 (T0-T3 at col 0, T4-T7 at col 1, etc.)
- The arrow in the figure indicates the direction of the %laneid:{fragments} axis spanning columns 0 through 7
mma-16832-B-i8_2.png:
MMA .m16n8k32 Fragment Layout for Rows 16-31 of Matrix B
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the fragment layout for the SECOND half (rows 16-31) of matrix B in the m16n8k32 MMA instruction. The layout is indexed by Row (groups of 4 matrix rows) and Column (0 through 7, with columns 2 and .. implied between 1 and 7).

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows represent groups of 4 matrix rows (16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31)
- Columns represent 8 column groups (0, 1, 2, .., 7)
- Each cell contains one thread holding 4 elements (b4, b5, b6, b7) packed into registers
- The label "%laneid:{fragments}" indicates thread assignment is by lane ID

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING TABLE:

Row Group 16-19:
  Col 0: T0  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T4  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied, pattern continues)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T28 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 20-23:
  Col 0: T1  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T5  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T29 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 24-27:
  Col 0: T2  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T6  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T30 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row Group 28-31:
  Col 0: T3  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 1: T7  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Col 2: (implied)
  ...:   (pattern continues)
  Col 7: T31 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover row groups 16-19, 20-23, 24-27, 28-31 respectively at column 0
- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column 1
- Threads T28-T31 cover the same row groups at column 7
- General pattern: Thread T(col*4 + row_group_index) handles the corresponding cell
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 16-19, 1 for 20-23, 2 for 24-27, 3 for 28-31
  and col ranges 0-7, giving thread numbers 0-31

ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING:
- Each thread holds elements named b4, b5, b6, b7 (these are the SECOND set of 4 elements, continuing from b0-b3 in rows 0-15)
- For 8-bit types (.u8, .s8, .e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1): four 8-bit values are packed into registers
- b4 and b5 are packed into one 32-bit register (two 8-bit values per 16-bit half, two halves per register)
- b6 and b7 are packed into a second 32-bit register
- This gives 2 registers per thread for this fragment (b4/b5 in reg0, b6/b7 in reg1), or all four may pack into a single 32-bit register depending on sub-byte packing

ARROW NOTATION:
- A diagonal arrow spans from column 2 row-group 28-31 up to column 7 row-group 16-19, indicating the continuation of the thread numbering pattern across the implied intermediate columns (2 through 6)

SUMMARY:
- Matrix: B, rows 16-31 of K dimension (second half of K=32)
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) each hold elements b4, b5, b6, b7
- Thread assignment: T(4*col + row_group) where col in [0..7], row_group in [0..3]
- Elements b4-b7 are the second quartet of B matrix register fragments per thread

- Row 4:  T16 (cols 0-1), T17 (cols 2-3), T18 (cols 4-5), T19 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 5:  T20 (cols 0-1), T21 (cols 2-3), T22 (cols 4-5), T23 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 6:  T24 (cols 0-1), T25 (cols 2-3), T26 (cols 4-5), T27 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 7:  T28 (cols 0-1), T29 (cols 2-3), T30 (cols 4-5), T31 (cols 6-7)

Lower half of matrix (rows 8-15):
- Row 8:  T0 (cols 0-1), T1 (cols 2-3), T2 (cols 4-5), T3 (cols 6-7)
- Row 9:  T4 (cols 0-1), T5 (cols 2-3), T6 (cols 4-5), T7 (cols 6-7)
- Row 10: T8 (cols 0-1), T9 (cols 2-3), T10 (cols 4-5), T11 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 11: T12 (cols 0-1), T13 (cols 2-3), T14 (cols 4-5), T15 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 12: T16 (cols 0-1), T17 (cols 2-3), T18 (cols 4-5), T19 (cols 6-7) [implied]
- Row 13: T20 (cols 0-1), T21 (cols 2-3), T22 (cols 4-5), T23 (cols 6-7) [implied]

- Row 15: T28 (cols 0-1), T29 (cols 2-3), T30 (cols 4-5), T31 (cols 6-7)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING:

Each thread holds 4 register elements total: c0, c1, c2, c3.

- Registers c0 and c1 cover the upper half of the matrix (rows 0-7) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Registers c2 and c3 cover the lower half of the matrix (rows 8-15) for that thread's assigned columns.
- Each thread entry shows {c0, c1} for rows 0-7 and {c2, c3} for rows 8-15, meaning two accumulator values are stored per thread per half.
- For .f16 type, two f16 values may be packed into a single 32-bit register.
- For .s32 and .f32 types, each element occupies one 32-bit register (c0, c1, c2, c3 are separate registers).

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY:

- 32 threads (T0-T31) cover the full 16x8 accumulator matrix.
- Each thread is responsible for exactly 2 columns and 2 rows (one in the upper half, one in the lower half), yielding 4 elements per thread total.
- Threads T0-T3 handle row 0; T4-T7 handle row 1; incrementing by 4 threads per row up to T28-T31 for row 7, then the pattern repeats for rows 8-15 with register indices c2,c3 instead of c0,c1.
- The arrows in rows 2 and 10 indicate the pattern continues (wrapping through T8-T27) between the explicitly shown rows.

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row group 0-7 (matrix rows 0 to 7):

Row group 8-15 (matrix rows 8 to 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 16-23 (matrix rows 16 to 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row group 24-31 (matrix rows 24 to 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, arrow from column 2 row 0-7 points down to column 2 row 24-31 indicating continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31 holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN (column index to thread number):
  Column 0: T0 (rows 0-7), T1 (rows 8-15), T2 (rows 16-23), T3 (rows 24-31)
  Column 1: T4 (rows 0-7), T5 (rows 8-15), T6 (rows 16-23), T7 (rows 24-31)
  Column 2: (T8, T9, T10, T11 implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 (rows 0-7), T29 (rows 8-15), T30 (rows 16-23), T31 (rows 24-31)

General pattern: Thread number = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index = 0 for rows 0-7, 1 for rows 8-15, 2 for rows 16-23, 3 for rows 24-31

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 8 elements per register entry: b0, b1, b2 (implied by ".."), b3, b4, b5 (implied), b6, b7.
For .u4/.s4 types: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
For .e2m1 type: 8 x 4-bit elements = 32 bits = one 32-bit register.
Elements b0 through b7 are packed sequentially within a single 32-bit register, with b0 in the least significant bits.

ARROW ANNOTATION:
A diagonal arrow in the figure spans from column 2 of row-group 0-7 down to column 2 of row-group 24-31, indicating that the pattern shown explicitly for columns 0, 1, and 7 continues uniformly across all 8 columns (0 through 7).
